name: dask-gateway

metadata.name: dask-gateway
metadata.summary: A client library for interacting with a dask-gateway server

documentation:
dask-gateway
============

A client library for interacting with a dask-gateway server. See `the
documentation <https://gateway.dask.org/>`__ for more information.

classes.name: dask_gateway.auth.GatewayAuth

methods.name: dask_gateway.auth.GatewayAuth.pre_request
documentation:
Authentication hook before client request is sent.

This is only called after a 401 response from the server.

# Parameters

resp

: The 401 response that triggered the authentication step.

# Returns

headers

: Any headers to add to the outgoing request.

context

: If necessary, may optionally return an opaque object storing
  additional context needed to complete the authentication process.
  This will be passed to `post_response`.

methods.name: dask_gateway.auth.GatewayAuth.post_response
documentation:
Authentication hook after server response is received.

# Parameters

resp

: The server response

context

: The return value of `pre_request`, providing any additional
  context needed to complete the authentication process.
Base class for authenticating clients in dask-gateway

classes.name: dask_gateway.options.Options
documentation:
Options to submit to dask-gateway.

A mutable-mapping that describes all cluster options available for user's
to set. Options can be modified programatically, or via a widget when using
a web interface (e.g. Jupyter Notebooks) with `ipywidgets` installed.

# Examples

Options objects are normally created via the `Gateway.cluster_options`
method:

```pycon
>>> options = gateway.cluster_options()  # doctest: +SKIP
```

Available options can then be accessed or set via attribute or key:

```pycon
>>> options.worker_cores  # doctest: +SKIP
1
>>> options["worker_cores"]  # doctest: +SKIP
1
>>> options.worker_cores = 2  # doctest: +SKIP
>>> options.worker_cores  # doctest: +SKIP
2
```

Accessing invalid options error appropriately:

```pycon
>>> options.not_a_valid_option = 'myvalue'  # doctest: +SKIP
Traceback (most recent call last):
    ...
AttributeError: No option 'not_a_valid_option' available
```

Errors are also raised if values being set are invalid (e.g. wrong type,
out-of-bounds, etc...).

classes.name: dask_gateway.client.GatewaySecurity
documentation:
A security implementation that temporarily stores credentials on disk.

The normal `Security` class assumes credentials already exist on disk,
but our credentials exist only in memory. Since Python's SSLContext doesn't
support directly loading credentials from memory, we write them temporarily
to disk when creating the context, then delete them immediately.

methods.name: dask_gateway.client._IntEnum.from_name
documentation: Create an enum value from a name

classes.name: dask_gateway.client.ClusterStatus
documentation:
The cluster's status.

Attributes
----------
PENDING : ClusterStatus
    The cluster is pending start.
RUNNING : ClusterStatus
    The cluster is running.
STOPPING : ClusterStatus
    The cluster is stopping, but not fully stopped.
STOPPED : ClusterStatus
    The cluster was shutdown by user or admin request.
FAILED : ClusterStatus
    A failure occurred during any of the above states, see the logs for
    more information.

classes.name: dask_gateway.client.ClusterReport

methods.name: dask_gateway.client.ClusterReport.security
documentation: A security object for this cluster, if present, else None

A report on a cluster's state.

Attributes
----------
name : str
    The cluster's name, a unique id.
options : dict
    User provided configuration for this cluster.
status : ClusterStatus
    The cluster's status.
scheduler_address : str or None
    The address of the scheduler, or None if not currently running.
dashboard_link : str or None
    A link to the dashboard, or None if not currently running.
start_time : datetime.datetime
    The time the cluster was started.
stop_time : datetime.datetime or None
    The time the cluster was stopped, or None if currently running.
tls_cert : str or None
    The tls certificate. None if not currently running or a full report
    wasn't requested.
tls_key : str or None
    The tls key. None if not currently running or a full report wasn't
    requested.

methods.name: dask_gateway.client.Gateway.close
documentation: Close the gateway client

methods.name: dask_gateway.client.Gateway.list_clusters
documentation:
List clusters for this user.

Parameters
----------
status : ClusterStatus, str, or list, optional
    The cluster status (or statuses) to select. Valid options are
    'pending', 'running', 'stopping', 'stopped', 'failed'.
    By default selects active clusters ('pending', 'running').

Returns
-------
clusters : list of ClusterReport

methods.name: dask_gateway.client.Gateway.get_cluster
documentation:
Get information about a specific cluster.

Parameters
----------
cluster_name : str
    The cluster name.

Returns
-------
report : ClusterReport

documentation:
Return version info for the server and client

Returns
-------
version_info : dict

methods.name: dask_gateway.client.Gateway.cluster_options
documentation:
Get the available cluster configuration options.

Parameters
----------
use_local_defaults : bool, optional
    Whether to use any default options from the local configuration.
    Default is True, set to False to use only the server-side defaults.

Returns
-------
cluster_options : dask_gateway.options.Options
    A dict of cluster options.

methods.name: dask_gateway.client.Gateway.submit
documentation:
Submit a new cluster to be started.

This returns quickly with a `cluster_name`, which can later be used
to connect to the cluster.

# Parameters

cluster_options

: An `Options` object describing the desired cluster configuration.

kwargs :

: Additional cluster configuration options. If `cluster_options` is
  provided, these are applied afterwards as overrides. Available
  options are specific to each deployment of dask-gateway, see
  `cluster_options` for more information.

# Returns

cluster_name

: The cluster name.

methods.name: dask_gateway.client.Gateway.connect
documentation:
Connect to a submitted cluster.

Parameters
----------
cluster_name : str
    The cluster to connect to.
shutdown_on_close : bool, optional
    If True, the cluster will be automatically shutdown on close.
    Default is False.

Returns
-------
cluster : GatewayCluster

methods.name: dask_gateway.client.Gateway.new_cluster
documentation:
Submit a new cluster to the gateway, and wait for it to be started.

Same as calling `submit` and `connect` in one go.

# Parameters

cluster_options

: An `Options` object describing the desired cluster configuration.

shutdown_on_close

: If True (default), the cluster will be automatically shutdown on
  close. Set to False to have cluster persist until explicitly
  shutdown.

kwargs :

: Additional cluster configuration options. If `cluster_options` is
  provided, these are applied afterwards as overrides. Available
  options are specific to each deployment of dask-gateway, see
  `cluster_options` for more information.

# Returns

cluster : GatewayCluster

methods.name: dask_gateway.client.Gateway.stop_cluster
documentation:
Stop a cluster.

Parameters
----------
cluster_name : str
    The cluster name.

methods.name: dask_gateway.client.Gateway.scale_cluster
documentation:
Scale a cluster to n workers.

Parameters
----------
cluster_name : str
    The cluster name.
n : int
    The number of workers to scale to.

methods.name: dask_gateway.client.Gateway.adapt_cluster
documentation:
Configure adaptive scaling for a cluster.

# Parameters

cluster_name

: The cluster name.

minimum

: The minimum number of workers to scale to. Defaults to 0.

maximum

: The maximum number of workers to scale to. Defaults to infinity.

active

: If `True` (default), adaptive scaling is activated. Set to
  `False` to deactivate adaptive scaling.

A client for a Dask Gateway Server.

# Parameters

address

: The address to the gateway server.

proxy_address

: The address of the scheduler proxy server. Defaults to `address` if not
  provided. If an int, it's used as the port, with the host/ip taken from
  `address`. Provide a full address if a different host/ip should be
  used.

public_address

: The address to the gateway server, as accessible from a web browser.
  This will be used as the root of all browser-facing links (e.g. the
  dask dashboard). Defaults to `address` if not provided.

auth

: The authentication method to use.

asynchronous

: If true, starts the client in asynchronous mode, where it can be used
  in other async code.

loop

: The IOLoop instance to use. Defaults to the current loop in
  asynchronous mode, otherwise a background loop is started.

classes.name: dask_gateway.client.GatewayCluster

methods.name: dask_gateway.client.GatewayCluster.from_name
documentation:
Connect to a submitted cluster.

# Parameters

cluster_name

: The cluster to connect to.

shutdown_on_close

: If True, the cluster will be automatically shutdown on close.
  Default is False.

kwargs :

: Additional parameters to pass to the `GatewayCluster`
  constructor. See the docstring for more information.

# Returns

cluster : GatewayCluster

methods.name: dask_gateway.client.GatewayCluster.close
documentation:
Close the cluster object.

# Parameters

shutdown

: Whether to shutdown the cluster. If True, the cluster will be
  shutdown, if False only the local state will be cleaned up.
  Defaults to the value of `shutdown_on_close`, set to True or
  False to override.

methods.name: dask_gateway.client.GatewayCluster.shutdown
documentation: Shutdown this cluster. Alias for `close(shutdown=True)`.

# Returns

methods.name: dask_gateway.client.GatewayCluster.scale
documentation:
Scale the cluster to `n` workers.

# Parameters

: The number of workers to scale to.

methods.name: dask_gateway.client.GatewayCluster.adapt
documentation:
Configure adaptive scaling for the cluster.

# Parameters

minimum

: The minimum number of workers to scale to. Defaults to 0.

maximum

: The maximum number of workers to scale to. Defaults to infinity.

active

: If `True` (default), adaptive scaling is activated. Set to
  `False` to deactivate adaptive scaling.

methods.name: dask_gateway.client.GatewayCluster._widget
documentation: Create IPython widget for display within a notebook

A dask-gateway cluster.

# Parameters

address

: The address to the gateway server.

proxy_address

: The address of the scheduler proxy server. If an int, it's used as the
  port, with the host/ip taken from `address`. Provide a full address
  if a different host/ip should be used.

public_address

: The address to the gateway server, as accessible from a web browser.
  This will be used as the root of all browser-facing links (e.g. the
  dask dashboard). Defaults to `address` if not provided.

auth

: The authentication method to use.

cluster_options

: A mapping of cluster options to use to start the cluster.

shutdown_on_close

: If True (default), the cluster will be automatically shutdown on
  close.

asynchronous

: If true, starts the cluster in asynchronous mode, where it can be used
  in other async code.

loop

: The IOLoop instance to use. Defaults to the current loop in
  asynchronous mode, otherwise a background loop is started.

kwargs :

: Additional cluster configuration options. If `cluster_options` is
  provided, these are applied afterwards as overrides. Available options
  are specific to each deployment of dask-gateway, see
  `Gateway.cluster_options` for more information.
